29.reason: [The loop breaks when exponent is 0, but the problem states that terms with zero coefficient are omitted, not that input ends with exponent 0. This causes premature termination if a term has exponent 0 (i.e., constant term) and misses remaining terms.]

38.reason: [Same error as above: incorrectly assumes input ends when exponent is 0, which is not the case per problem description. Input ends with newline, not a zero exponent.]

47.reason: [Loop bounds are off-by-one: should be i1 < i and j1 < j because i and j point to the first unused index after reading, not inclusive of the last valid index. This accesses uninitialized data (including the zero-exponent term that caused break).]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop bounds are off-by-one: should be i1 < i and j1 < j because i and j point to the first unused index after reading, not inclusive of the last valid index. This accesses uninitialized data (including the zero-exponent term that caused break).]

96.reason: [The merging logic is flawed: it only merges consecutive duplicates and stops at j < n-1, so the last element is never merged. Also, it doesn't handle non-consecutive duplicates because array isn't guaranteed to have all same exponents adjacent before full sort (but sort is descending, so maybe okay). However, primary issue is j < n-1 should be j < n to include last element.]
